_comment: Dead-man heartbeat for the 15-min options-automation cron (HARD RULE 8). Stamped once per trading day by the first automation run of that day. No stamp today = the automation is dead; open a session to re-arm.
note: Options automation run (routine-triggered, market open Wed 09:30 ET). EXITS: get_option_positions shows exactly 1 open position -- ACHR 2028-01-21 5C (LEAP, entry $2.47, mark ~$2.59, +5%) -- which carries a manual_hold_override (set_utc 2026-07-22T14:30:00Z, Ryan live-turn quote 'I'll handle the achr leap. Just leave it be', added via interactive session/PR #66) suspending all mechanical exits (-50%/TP/DTE) and explicitly asking not to be re-litigated in alerts -- skipped entirely, no action, no re-notification. RBRK 90C (the other position from 07-21) is already closed: mechanical -50% cut fired correctly in a prior run (sell-to-close 2 contracts @ $1.05, 2026-07-21T16:31:55Z) -- confirmed via get_option_orders, no longer in get_option_positions or holdings.json. ENTRIES: skipped -- both latest_morning.md and latest_intraday.md on master are still stamped 2026-07-21 (no fresh report yet today), so no new technical signal to gate an entry against. Options sleeve is back under cap (ACHR only, ~$259 mark / ~7% of ~$3684 account) but that's moot without a fresh signal -- any doubt = skip per HARD RULE 8. Realized options P&L today: $0. Daily loss cap (-$150) not hit. No new or unresolved _flag entries in holdings.json this run (checked); the ACHR manual_hold_override is the only special-handling item and is unchanged from the prior run, so not re-flagged per the HARD RULE 9 no-re-notify-on-unchanged-state principle.
